To find the **greatest common divisor (GCD)** of two numbers, we can use the **Euclidean algorithm**.

We are given:
$$
a = 106029, \quad b = 38115
$$

### Step 1: Apply the Euclidean algorithm
We repeatedly apply the division algorithm:
$$
106029 = 38115 \cdot 2 + 30000 - 2 \cdot 38115 = 38115 \cdot 2 + 30000 - 2 \cdot 38115 = 38115 \cdot 2 + 30000 - 2 \cdot 38115
$$
$$
106029 = 38115 \cdot 2 + 30000 - 2 \cdot 38115 = 38115 \cdot 2 + 30000 - 2 \cdot 38115
$$
$$
\text{Remainder} = 106029 \mod 38115 = 106029 - 2 \cdot 38115 = 106029 - 76230 = 29799
$$

Now compute:
$$
38115 \mod 29799 = 38115 - 29799 = 8316
$$

Now compute:
$$
29799 \mod 8316 = 29799 - 3 \cdot 8316 = 29799 - 24948 = 4851
$$

Now compute:
$$
8316 \mod 4851 = 8316 - 1 \cdot 4851 = 3465
$$

Now compute:
$$
4851 \mod 3465 = 4851 - 1 \cdot 3465 = 1386
$$
